name: Masamune Blades
description: The Masamune Blades, forged by the legendary swordsmith Gorō Nyūdō Masamune, represent the pinnacle of Japanese sword-making artistry and spiritual purity. Masamune, believed to have lived from the late 13th to early 14th century, was renowned for imbuing his blades with peace and tranquility. Unlike his contemporary Muramasa, whose swords were said to crave blood, Masamune's blades were believed to possess a virtuous nature, refusing to be drawn unless for righteous purposes. The exact number of swords Masamune forged is unknown, shrouded in legend and historical ambiguity. Many tales recount duels between Masamune and Muramasa's swords, often ending with Masamune's blade sheathing itself after sensing the innocence of its would-be victim, while Muramasa's continued its destructive path. These stories highlight the contrasting philosophies of the two smiths, portraying Masamune as a master of balance and harmony. Masamune's swords are often considered national treasures, symbols of skill, artistry, and the samurai ethos of justice and honor. They are not merely weapons but works of art, embodying the spiritual and philosophical values of their creator. The stories surrounding Masamune's blades are integral to understanding the cultural significance of swords in Japanese society and the importance of the swordsmith's role.
subtitle: Swords of peace and unparalleled skill

keyMyths.title: The Duel of the Blades
keyMyths.description: A famous story tells of a contest between Masamune and Muramasa, where their blades were tested by being placed in a stream. Masamune's blade did not cut innocent passersby, while Muramasa's continued to cut everything in its path.

keyMyths.title: The Virtue of the Sword
keyMyths.description: Masamune's swords are believed to possess a virtuous nature, refusing to be drawn unless for righteous purposes. This contrasts sharply with the bloodthirsty reputation of Muramasa's blades.

sources.author: Kanzan Sato
sources.description: Provides historical and technical information about Japanese swords.
sources.text: Provides historical and technical information about Japanese swords.

sources.author: Yoshikawa Eiji
sources.description: Fictionalized account of Miyamoto Musashi's life, referencing famous swordsmiths.
